metadata.title: Assemble & Ship the PDF
metadata.source: INFO 7375 Assignment 9 brief — Making Your Brand Unforgettable

beats.narration_text: Hola — this is Liam. Episode 6 of 6. One PDF with specific contents, a specific name, and a final voice check before you export. The points that are easiest to lose in this assignment are the mechanical ones — word count missing, framework label missing, article URL not clickable, promo screenshot not in the PDF. This episode is a checklist. Run through it on camera if you are recording a demo, or run through it in your head if you are not. Either way, run through it. Let's close the assignment.

props.command: One PDF: LastName_FirstName_Assignment9.pdf. All required elements in order. Final voice check before export.
props.runningText: the easy-to-lose points are mechanical — word count, framework label, clickable URL, promo screenshot…
output: 3 × 100-word arcs with framework labels and word counts
500-word article: clickable URL + screenshot + promo post screenshot + custom graphic

beats.narration_text: The PDF checklist. Name the file: LastName_FirstName_Assignment9.pdf. Part 1: Brand Stories. The three 100-word arcs, each with the framework name as a visible header and a word count at the bottom. One hundred words is a target — ninety to one hundred twenty is acceptable, but the word count must be visible. Then the 300-word expanded story, also with a word count. Five pieces total in Part 1. Part 2: The Article. The article URL — must be clickable in the PDF, not just pasted as plain text. A screenshot of the published article on the platform, showing the title, graphic, and first paragraph. A screenshot of the LinkedIn promo post. And the custom graphic, embedded at full size so it is legible, not a thumbnail. Four items in Part 2. Check them in order. Missing word count on the 100-word arcs: easy to forget, easy to lose points. Non-clickable URL: paste it as a hyperlink, not plain text. Promo screenshot not in the PDF: screenshot the LinkedIn post after you publish it, before you submit. Graphic too small to read: embed it at a size where the content is legible at normal zoom.

artifactLines: Part 1 — Brand Stories: 3 × 100-word arcs (framework label + word count each) + 300-word story (word count).
Part 2 — The Article: clickable URL · published article screenshot (title + graphic + first paragraph).
· LinkedIn promo post screenshot · custom graphic at legible size.
Easy-to-lose: word count missing · URL not clickable · promo screenshot not included · graphic too small.
props.sparkLine: Word count. Clickable URL. Promo screenshot. Graphic legible. All 5 items in Part 1, all 4 in Part 2.

beats.narration_text: The final voice check. Before you export the PDF, read one of the three arcs out loud to someone who has not seen your work. Do not tell them which framework it is. Do not tell them anything about the assignment. Just read it. Then ask them two questions. First: what was that about? If their answer could describe three other people's stories, the story is not specific enough yet — there is still a generic element that needs to be replaced with a concrete detail that only applies to you. Second: did it sound like a person or like a LinkedIn post? If they say 'LinkedIn post,' that is the voice that needs to come out. One more rewrite. The assignment says 'sound like a human, not a LinkedIn post generator' — that is the standard, not a suggestion. If all three arcs are already in your own voice and a stranger can summarize each one in a sentence that does not apply to the others, you are done. Export the PDF. Submit. The whole point of this assignment is that the stories you write here — the 100-word arcs, the expanded story, the article — are portfolio pieces. They live on your About page and your LinkedIn and your next job application. Write them as if that is where they are going, because that is exactly where they are going.

props.artifactHeading: Read one arc aloud to someone who hasn't seen it
artifactLines: Ask: 'What was that about?' — could the answer describe 3 other people's stories? Not specific enough.
Ask: 'Did it sound like a person or a LinkedIn post?' — if LinkedIn: one more rewrite.
Standard: human voice, not LinkedIn post generator. That's the bar.
When done: all 3 arcs in your voice, each summable in 1 sentence that doesn't fit the others.
These are portfolio pieces — About page, LinkedIn, job applications. Write them like that.
props.sparkLine: Pass both tests. Then export, submit, and they're your portfolio.

beats.narration_text: Verdict for Assemble and Ship. One PDF named LastName_FirstName_Assignment9.pdf. Part 1: three 100-word arcs with framework labels and word counts, one 300-word story with word count. Part 2: clickable article URL, screenshot of the published article, screenshot of the LinkedIn promo post, and the custom graphic at legible size. Final voice check before export: read one arc to someone who has not seen it, ask what it was about, ask if it sounded like a person. Pass both tests, then export. The stories written here are portfolio pieces — they go on About pages, LinkedIn summaries, and job applications. Write them like that, because that is where they are going.

artifactLines: Part 1: 3 × 100-word arcs (label + word count) + 300-word story (word count).
Part 2: clickable URL · article screenshot · promo post screenshot · custom graphic (legible).
Easy-to-lose: word count · clickable URL · promo screenshot · graphic size.
Final voice check: read aloud → specific? · sounds like a person?
These are portfolio pieces. Write them like that.

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Run this pre-submit checklist before you export the PDF. Go through it item by item. Part 1 — Brand Stories: do all three arcs have the framework name as a visible header? Do all three have a word count at the bottom? Is the 300-word story labeled and does it have a word count? Is the word count between two hundred fifty and three hundred fifty? Part 2 — The Article: is the URL in the PDF a live hyperlink, not plain text? Does clicking it open the article in a browser? Is there a screenshot of the published article showing the title, the graphic, and the first paragraph? Is there a screenshot of the LinkedIn promo post — the actual post, not a draft? Is the custom graphic embedded at a size where the content is legible? Final check: read one arc aloud to someone who has not seen it. Ask them two questions: what was that about, and did it sound like a person or a LinkedIn post? If the answer to either is wrong, do one more rewrite. If both pass, export the file as LastName_FirstName_Assignment9.pdf and submit to Canvas. That is the complete assignment.

props.command: Pre-submit checklist: Part 1 — 3 arcs have framework labels? Word counts on all 4 pieces? Part 2 — URL is a live hyperlink? Article screenshot included? Promo post screenshot included? Graphic legible? Final voice check: read aloud → specific? sounds like a person? Both pass → export as LastName_FirstName_Assignment9.pdf → submit.
props.runningText: if either voice-check question fails — one more rewrite, then export; these go on your About page…

beats.narration_text: Assemble & Ship the PDF. Liam, in for Bear.
